name: Goddard Lieberson
description: British-born American composer; record producer, recording industry professional
abstract: Goddard Lieberson (April 5, 1911 – May 29, 1977) was the president of Columbia Records from 1956 to 1971, and again from 1973 to 1975. He became president of the Recording Industry Association of America in 1964.

about_quotes.speaker: Iola Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: Goddard Lieberson liked the concept and he said he thought he could sell it to the people on the west coast maybe, and he took “Take Five” and went out to sales meeting on the west coast
about_quotes.doc_title: Iola Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Wilton, CT, July 17, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: The head of Columbia Records was Goddard Lieberson, worked in the English music halls. He played piano and would do a dance for you. He was an artist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: the man, Goddard Lieberson, who was head of the serious department... I'm glad I had the good fortune to be associated with some of these people.

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: when I was recording for Columbia, the head of Columbia was Goddard Lieberson... had the sympathy and the dedication to try to promote the arts
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: I know Goddard Lieberson gets all the publicity, but Goddard was still the vice president in charge of the classical department.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: They’ve dropped me, and yet Goddard has begged me to come and play... we had done a good job for Columbia over the years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Brubeck — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
